methods.method_name: Molecular and Genetic Analysis of BET11/12
methods.description: This method describes the selection of candidate genes and mutant lines, followed by molecular analysis of protein sequences, genotyping of plant lines, and initial phenotypic assessment of single and double mutants.

steps.description: From a screen for potential gametophytic mutants, we chose the Arabidopsis At3g58170 and At4g14455 loci (BET11 and BET12) for study (Additional file 1: Table S1). Then from a subset of 18 mutant lines corresponding to 12 SNARE genes, we chose the Columbia T-DNA lines SAIL_509_C09 (bet11-1) and SALK_124063 (bet12-1) for study. Genes were selected by high expression in Arabidopsis pollen as reported by GENEVESTIGATOR (https://www.genevestigator.com).
steps.searchable_summary: Select candidate genes and specific T-DNA mutant lines based on screening results and expression data from databases.
inputs: Results from screen for gametophytic mutants
Subset of 18 mutant lines for 12 SNARE genes

steps.description: The respective amino acid sequences were analyzed by T-Coffee, a consistencybased multiple sequence alignment program (Di Tommaso et al. 2011) and the on-line repositories UNIPROT (http://www.uniprot.org) and SWISS-MODEL (swissmodel.expasy.org).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze protein amino acid sequences using multiple sequence alignment software and online protein databases.
inputs: Amino acid sequences of BET11 and BET12

steps.description: Plants from each line were genotyped with specific primer pairs for their corresponding T-DNA inserts and WT locus.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform genotyping of plant lines using PCR with specific primers for T-DNA insertions and wild-type loci.
inputs: Specific primer pairs (for T-DNA inserts, for WT locus)
outputs: Genotyping results (e.g., presence/absence of T-DNA, zygosity)

steps.description: Single homozygous lines did not show obvious vegetative or reproductive phenotypes, but homozygous/heterozygous (HM/HZ) double mutants for bet11-1 and bet12-1 (and vice versa) showed reduced seed set. No double HM mutants were recovered.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess vegetative, reproductive, and seed set phenotypes in single homozygous and double mutant (HM/HZ, HM/HM) plant lines.
inputs: Double mutant lines (bet11-1/bet11-1; bet12-1/+, bet11-1/+; bet12-1/bet12-1, bet11-1/bet11-1; bet12-1/bet12-1)
outputs: Phenotypic observations (e.g., vegetative growth, reproductive development, seed set)
